title: Translation-readiness: text/figure separation + localization export
context: To enable translation of the explainers into other languages (synergy with the oncology-glossary-multilingual sibling project), explainer text must be separated from figure code so a localizer can translate the prose strings without touching the statistical computation. After string substitution, the same figure scripts should regenerate with the translated labels. This M3 task follows release-019 and makes the library translation-ready without requiring a full re-authoring effort for each language.
objective: Refactor the explainer content structure to separate translatable text strings from figure/computation code, and produce a localization-ready export (e.g. a JSON or YAML message file per explainer) that a translator can work with — while ensuring figures regenerate correctly from the same scripts after string substitution.
acceptanceCriteria: Translatable text strings (plain definitions, precise definitions, common trap text, checklist items, figure axis labels, alt-text, framing notices) are extracted from each explainer into a localization-ready export file (e.g. JSON or YAML) keyed by string ID.

Figure generation scripts are refactored to accept translatable string parameters (axis labels, titles, legends) rather than having them hardcoded, so figures regenerate with localized strings after substitution.
The English locale is committed as the reference locale; the export format is documented so a translator can contribute a new locale file without touching computation code.
The reproducibility harness (repro-005) continues to pass after the refactor; figures regenerated from the English locale strings match the pre-refactor checksums.
The export format and translation contribution workflow are documented in the project contributing guide (with a note about synergy with oncology-glossary-multilingual).
MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; CI green; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: tasks/stats-for-clinicians-repro-005.json — reproducibility harness (must still pass after refactor)
oncology-glossary-multilingual sibling project — potential consumer of the localization export
output: A merged MIT-licensed PR that separates translatable text from figure code, produces a localization-ready export for each explainer, and ensures CI and reproducibility still pass. The English reference locale is committed and the contribution workflow is documented.
